title: Do not hardcode secrets or long-lived credentials
text: Hardcoded tokens, passwords, API keys, and similar credentials create rotation, exposure, and audit problems. Prefer runtime-provided secrets with narrowly scoped access.
summary: Secret-like literals should live in secret management or deployment configuration, not in source.
embedding_text:
rule_no: SEC.1
pack_id: shared_security
language_id: shared
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: SEC
title: Do not hardcode secrets or long-lived credentials
summary: Secret-like literals should live in secret management or deployment configuration, not in source.
keywords: not, secrets, credentials, sec.1, hardcode, long-lived, secret-like, literals, should, live, secret, management
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: hardcoded_secret
fix_guidance: Move the secret into environment-backed configuration or a secret manager and inject it at runtime.
text: Hardcoded tokens, passwords, API keys, and similar credentials create rotation, exposure, and audit problems. Prefer runtime-provided secrets with narrowly scoped access.
fix_guidance: Move the secret into environment-backed configuration or a secret manager and inject it at runtime.

title: Build shell commands from trusted primitives, not concatenated strings
text: Command construction through string concatenation is fragile and exposes injection risk. Prefer argument vectors, explicit allow-lists, or dedicated APIs.
summary: Shell execution paths should not interpolate untrusted fragments into a command string.
embedding_text:
rule_no: SEC.2
pack_id: shared_security
language_id: shared
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: SEC
title: Build shell commands from trusted primitives, not concatenated strings
summary: Shell execution paths should not interpolate untrusted fragments into a command string.
keywords: shell, not, command, sec.2, build, commands, trusted, primitives, concatenated, strings, execution, paths
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: shell_injection
fix_guidance: Use structured subprocess or exec APIs that separate the executable from validated arguments.
text: Command construction through string concatenation is fragile and exposes injection risk. Prefer argument vectors, explicit allow-lists, or dedicated APIs.
fix_guidance: Use structured subprocess or exec APIs that separate the executable from validated arguments.

title: Parameterize SQL instead of composing query text dynamically
text: SQL should be parameterized through the database driver's bind facilities or a higher-level abstraction. Dynamic text assembly should be reserved for carefully controlled structural clauses.
summary: Query text built from variables is difficult to reason about and easy to misuse.
embedding_text:
rule_no: SEC.3
pack_id: shared_security
language_id: shared
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: SEC
title: Parameterize SQL instead of composing query text dynamically
summary: Query text built from variables is difficult to reason about and easy to misuse.
keywords: text, sql, query, should, sec.3, parameterize, instead, composing, dynamically, built, variables, difficult
category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: sql_injection, dynamic_sql
fix_guidance: Bind values separately from the SQL string and constrain structural query changes through allow-listed fragments.
text: SQL should be parameterized through the database driver's bind facilities or a higher-level abstraction. Dynamic text assembly should be reserved for carefully controlled structural clauses.
fix_guidance: Bind values separately from the SQL string and constrain structural query changes through allow-listed fragments.

title: Do not leave fallback secrets or test credentials in production code paths
text: Even when intended only for local development or bootstrap, embedded fallback secrets create a second and weaker trust path that is easy to forget during incident response or key rotation.
summary: Backup credentials often become the least-audited credential in the system.

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: hardcoded_secret
fix_guidance: Remove embedded fallback credentials and require runtime configuration or a dedicated local-only development path instead.
text: Even when intended only for local development or bootstrap, embedded fallback secrets create a second and weaker trust path that is easy to forget during incident response or key rotation.
fix_guidance: Remove embedded fallback credentials and require runtime configuration or a dedicated local-only development path instead.

title: Keep command execution APIs structured so the executable and arguments remain distinct
text: Separating the executable from validated arguments narrows the injection surface and makes the trust boundary visible in code review across languages and runtimes.
summary: Security review is much stronger when command construction never depends on ad hoc shell parsing.

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: shell_injection
fix_guidance: Use structured process APIs, validate each argument according to its contract, and avoid passing shell fragments through string concatenation.
text: Separating the executable from validated arguments narrows the injection surface and makes the trust boundary visible in code review across languages and runtimes.

title: Dynamic query structure should stay allow-listed even when values are parameterized
text: ORDER BY fragments, table identifiers, and optional clauses can still create injection or correctness risk when the allowed structures are not tightly bounded.
summary: Parameter binding protects values, not uncontrolled structural SQL changes.

title: Example, bootstrap, and fallback credentials in committed code should be treated as real secrets unless clearly impossible to activate
text: Backup and bootstrap secrets often become the least-audited authentication path in a system because everyone assumes they are temporary or unreachable.
summary: "Test" or "dev" labels do not remove rotation, misuse, or confusion risk once credentials are committed.

category: security
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: hardcoded_secret
fix_guidance: Remove committed fallback credentials or move them into an isolated local-only development path that cannot ship as a production default.

title: Close newly opened resources promptly and visibly
text: In Go, defer is most useful when it is placed immediately after the operation that acquires the resource. Delayed cleanup registration is easy to miss in review.
summary: Resource cleanup should usually be attached immediately after successful acquisition.

category: resource_management
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: defer_close_missing
fix_guidance: Register deferred cleanup immediately after checking acquisition success.
text: In Go, defer is most useful when it is placed immediately after the operation that acquires the resource. Delayed cleanup registration is easy to miss in review.
fix_guidance: Register deferred cleanup immediately after checking acquisition success.

title: Propagate context through request-scoped work
text: Context propagation keeps cancellation, timeout, and traceability behavior coherent across concurrent and networked operations.
summary: Request cancellation and deadlines should follow the work they govern.

category: error_handling
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: context_missing, goroutine_leak
fix_guidance: Thread context into new work units or document why the new path is intentionally detached.
text: Context propagation keeps cancellation, timeout, and traceability behavior coherent across concurrent and networked operations.
fix_guidance: Thread context into new work units or document why the new path is intentionally detached.

title: Do not let error-returning calls disappear into unused control flow
text: If an operation returns an error, reviewers should be able to see immediately whether it is handled, wrapped, or intentionally ignored.
summary: Go readability depends on checking and propagating errors consistently.

category: error_handling
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: ignored_error
fix_guidance: Check the error directly after the call and return, wrap, or justify an ignore branch explicitly.
text: If an operation returns an error, reviewers should be able to see immediately whether it is handled, wrapped, or intentionally ignored.

title: Every goroutine should have a clear owner, lifetime, and cancellation story
text: Goroutines are cheap enough to start casually and expensive enough to debug later when they outlive the request, worker, or channel assumptions that created them.
summary: Detached concurrent work becomes a leak when nothing defines when it should stop.
embedding_text:
rule_no: GO.4
pack_id: project_go
language_id: go
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: GO
title: Every goroutine should have a clear owner, lifetime, and cancellation story
summary: Detached concurrent work becomes a leak when nothing defines when it should stop.
keywords: when, should, enough, go.4, every, goroutine, have, clear, owner, lifetime, cancellation, story
category: concurrency
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: goroutine_leak
fix_guidance: Tie goroutine lifetime to a context, channel closure, worker pool, or another explicit ownership boundary.
text: Goroutines are cheap enough to start casually and expensive enough to debug later when they outlive the request, worker, or channel assumptions that created them.
fix_guidance: Tie goroutine lifetime to a context, channel closure, worker pool, or another explicit ownership boundary.

title: Preserve context across request-scoped outbound work
text: Network calls, storage operations, and goroutine handoffs should usually inherit the context of the work they are serving unless the code is deliberately creating an independent background task.
summary: Lost context breaks timeout, cancellation, and trace boundaries in exactly the places reviewers care about most.
embedding_text:

language_id: go
context_id:

summary: Lost context breaks timeout, cancellation, and trace boundaries in exactly the places reviewers care about most.
keywords: context, work, go.5, preserve, across, request-scoped, outbound, lost, breaks, timeout, cancellation, trace
category: error_handling
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: context_missing
fix_guidance: Thread the caller context into the next operation or document why the work is intentionally decoupled from request lifetime.
text: Network calls, storage operations, and goroutine handoffs should usually inherit the context of the work they are serving unless the code is deliberately creating an independent background task.
fix_guidance: Thread the caller context into the next operation or document why the work is intentionally decoupled from request lifetime.

title: Register deferred cleanup immediately after successful acquisition
text: Review Go resource management so the success check and deferred cleanup remain adjacent. That pattern is easier to maintain when more branches are added later.
summary: Delayed defer placement makes later edits more likely to skip cleanup on new exit paths.

fix_guidance: Place the defer directly after the successful open, dial, or create call unless ownership is intentionally transferred elsewhere.
text: Review Go resource management so the success check and deferred cleanup remain adjacent. That pattern is easier to maintain when more branches are added later.

title: Wrap or surface errors with the operation context that failed
text: Go error handling is most useful when the next layer can tell which operation failed without reconstructing the path from logs or stack traces.
summary: Barely handled errors are hard to distinguish once they leave the local scope.

language_id: go
context_id:

category: error_handling
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: ignored_error
fix_guidance: Return the error promptly and add the operation context needed to understand or recover from the failure.
text: Go error handling is most useful when the next layer can tell which operation failed without reconstructing the path from logs or stack traces.
fix_guidance: Return the error promptly and add the operation context needed to understand or recover from the failure.

title: Detached background contexts should be exceptional in request-scoped code
text: A new root context is a lifecycle decision. In service and worker code it should usually be obvious why the work no longer belongs to the caller's cancellation boundary.
summary: Replacing caller context with Background or TODO often discards exactly the cancellation and deadline semantics the caller expected.

keywords: caller, background, should, code, context, cancellation, go.8, detached, contexts, exceptional, request-scoped, replacing
category: error_handling
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: context_background
fix_guidance: Prefer the incoming context unless the work is intentionally detached and that decision is documented through a dedicated background-work boundary.
text: A new root context is a lifecycle decision. In service and worker code it should usually be obvious why the work no longer belongs to the caller's cancellation boundary.
fix_guidance: Prefer the incoming context unless the work is intentionally detached and that decision is documented through a dedicated background-work boundary.

title: Panic should not replace typed error propagation in normal service or library paths
text: In Go, panic is easier to justify at process boundaries and invariant violations than in request or reusable library logic where callers expect errors.
summary: A panic is a crash contract, not just a shorter error-handling syntax.
embedding_text:
rule_no: GO.9
pack_id: project_go
language_id: go
context_id: 
dialect_id: 
section: GO
title: Panic should not replace typed error propagation in normal service or library paths
summary: A panic is a crash contract, not just a shorter error-handling syntax.
keywords: panic, not, library, go.9, should, replace, typed, error, propagation, normal, service, paths
category: error_handling
reviewability: auto_review
trigger_patterns: panic_call
fix_guidance: Return a typed error or convert the failure into a bounded error result unless the code is truly at a crash boundary or invariant check.
text: In Go, panic is easier to justify at process boundaries and invariant violations than in request or reusable library logic where callers expect errors.
fix_guidance: Return a typed error or convert the failure into a bounded error result unless the code is truly at a crash boundary or invariant check.

title: Anonymous goroutine launches still need an owner, stop condition, and error strategy
text: Inline goroutines can hide ownership and cancellation decisions from review, especially when they capture request-scoped values or channels implicitly.
summary: Concurrency leaks are not safer just because the goroutine is written inline.

title: Sentinel error branches should stay stable when errors gain wrapping context
text: Go code often adds context with `%w` or passes errors across package boundaries. `errors.Is` and `errors.As` keep sentinel and typed-error branches working when the original error is wrapped on the way back to the caller.
summary: Direct `err == ErrX` checks are brittle once helpers start returning wrapped errors.
